title: Upgrade, CONNECT, Tunnels, WebSocket Boundaries, and Safe Transitions · Language Lens: Buffered Handoff
summary: A client sends new-protocol bytes before acceptance, a server parses tunneled data as another HTTP request, buffered bytes disappear during handoff, and rejected transitions desynchronize the connection. Planned artifact: Add and verify the buffered handoff increment in confirmed protocol-transition and buffered-byte handoff state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Transfer connection, buffered unread octets, deadlines, close ownership, and cancellation atomically to the selected protocol or tunnel.
Parse CONNECT authority-form, validate host and port policy, authenticate proxy use, establish the target, then transition after a successful response.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Buffered Handoff · Theory Buffered Handoff
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 7997 in a privacy-sensitive proxy translating between two parser implementations. Build deterministic Go evidence to wait for confirmed Upgrade or CONNECT acceptance before transferring parser ownership; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Buffered Handoff contract for the supplied data.
